titlestmt.titleproper: Department of Household Economics and Management Research studies23-18-1648
titlestmt.author: Compiled by RMC Staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2020-11-29 18:09:52 -0500.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: Department of Household Economics and Management Research studies

origination.label: creator
origination.corpname: New York State College of Home Economics. Department of Household Economics and Management

did.abstract: New York State College of Home Economics Department of Household Economics and Management Research studies

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

userestrict.head: Restrictions on Use:
userestrict.p: Access is granted with the understanding that names of individual respondents will not be published or otherwise released in such form that any individual mentioned in the records can be identified.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Department of Household Economics and Management Research studies, #23-18-1648. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Specific surveys include: management of family finances, Livingston County, New York, 1928-29; cost of living for farm families, Tompkins County, New York, 1928-29; economic contributions of household members, New York State, 1960-61; financial management practices of young families, Tompkins County, New York, 1955-58; financial management in relation to family values and concepts of financial security, Norwich, New York, 1961; financial management of graduate women, Cornell University, 1955; financial management from marriage to 1935, Tompkins and Livingston Counties, New York, 1935; food shopping practices of families, Cleveland, Ohio and Rochester, New York, 1939; kinds and amounts of representative foods used during one week, Cleveland, Ohio and Rochester, New York, 1938; management of food purchases and meals, New York State, 1959; food consumption of farm and village families, central New York State, 1927; inventories of outerwear of farm families, Tompkins County, New York, 1950; types of equipment found in homes, Buffalo, New York, 1949; repair of small household electrical appliances, Canandaigua, New York, 1956; use of small kitchen equipment by young families, Ithaca, New York and vicinity, 1936; use of carpentry tools as a home management resource, New York State, 1945; major equipment work space, and storage space in homes, Ithaca, New York and vicinity, 1936-42; acceptance of the mechanical dishwasher, Rochester, New York, 1955-56; laundry practices and equipment, Erie, Pennsylvania, 1959; and family laundry practices and costs, Tompkins County, New York, 1945.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Also, buying and selling potatoes in retail groceries, Cleveland, Ohio and Rochester, New York, 1937-38; price variation in retail grocery stores, New York State, 1935; availability and price of fruits and vegetables in retail stores, Ithaca, New York, 1942-45; store practices and the sale of washing machines on credit, New York State, 1938-40; size of purchasing centers of families, New York State, 1928; a study of credit bureaus, New York State, 1940-42; decision-making concerning household activities, New York State, 1957; daily activities of homemakers confined to wheel chairs, Ithaca, New York and vicinity, 1954; distance traveled in farm homes when preparing and serving food and washing dishes, Seneca County, New York, 1949-51; financial education at the secondary school level, Sherburne, New York, 1949; and selection of heat for cooking, New York, 1953.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence, surveys, publications and class projects, concerning various courses taught in the dept., including work that contributed to the President's Conference on Home Building and Home Ownership in 1931and photographs of kitchens from homes in the local area.
